title: Golden Buttery Spanish Corn Croquetas
description: Buttery, cheese-pull, Spanish dish featuring dried white corn, perfect for a low-carb snack or meal prep option. This Galician-inspired recipe is a delicious and satisfying treat.
instructions: Soak the dried white corn in water overnight and drain well.
In a large bowl, combine the soaked corn, melted butter, shredded cheese, beaten egg, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Mix well until all the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small cylindrical croquetas.
Roll each croqueta in almond flour to coat.
Place the coated croquetas on a plate or tray and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.
Heat about 1-2 inches of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees.
Carefully place the croquetas in the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the croquetas from the oil with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the golden buttery Spanish corn croquetas warm and enjoy.
